label: Codex Regius Main Scribe
hand_type: Gothic book hand with insular features
training_context: Icelandic scriptorium tradition, c. mid-13th century

biography.name: Unknown (single professional main hand)
biography.career_summary: Trained professional copyist; highly consistent letterforms across the Poetic Edda portion of GKS 2365 4to. No colophon survives; identity remains scholarly open question.
research_placeholders: Possible connection to Norwegian exemplar tradition

timeline.event: Oral and written Eddic tradition circulating in Iceland/Norway
Probable terminus post quem for exemplar traditions behind Codex Regius
Main scribe copies Poetic Edda onto prepared vellum
Marginalia and minor corrections (same or secondary hand — pending page-level analysis)
Probable terminus ante quem for primary copying phase
Bishop Brynjólfur Sveinsson presents codex to Frederick III of Denmark; named Codex Regius
Returned to Iceland; Árni Magnússon Institute stewardship

traits: Possible second hand — verify per page

cross_manuscript_hands.relation: Prose Edda parallels; different scribal team
Indirect; prose vs poetic copying norms
